column6: There are TWO points here for consideration: 1) The Feminine Form listed indicated here by: "-tā", might be considered for listing as an Official Headword, thus: "kāṅkṣitā". LB 2) Apparently, according to MW Dictionary, the Feminine Form "kāṅkṣitā" is Derived from: "kāṅkṣin + tā". This is why MW indents the Headword "kāṅkṣitā" Directly Below the Headword "kāṅkṣin". Therefore, if you choose to Officially List the Apte Headword "kāṅkṣitā", so as to be a "Searchable" Headword, you may consider the above point so as to Locate it Correctly. Andhrabharati has mentioned that APTE has a particular method for organizing his Dictionary, but should idiosyncratic methodology, overrule Sanskrit vyākaraṇam ?
